content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves sharply to the left ahead with the dashed center line sweeping left, and the car appears to be on the inside of the curve, slightly left of center but on track.

Target waypoint: nearest_waypoints[1] + 5 = 25 + 5 = 30
Waypoint 30: (7.230445, 1.417204)
Car position: (6.623321, 0.819633)
Bearing = atan2(1.417204 - 0.819633, 7.230445 - 6.623321) = atan2(0.597571, 0.607124) = atan2(0.9843) ≈ 44.5°
Current heading: 19.168°
Delta = 44.5 - 19.168 = 25.33° → moderate-left turn needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves sharply left ahead; delta to waypoint 30 is +25.33° (left), so I apply 15° left steering at speed 1.5 to follow the curve without oversteering.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track starts with a long straight section heading in +X direction along y≈0.683",
    "Large left-curving loop begins around waypoint 24-30 (y increases rapidly)",
    "Car is slightly left of center (0.055m) but all wheels on track — acceptable",
    "Use steering_angle=15 for delta 20-30° entering curve at speed 1.5",
    "After the left curve, track loops back and returns — full circuit ~17.7m",
    "Speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for straight sections and moderate curves",
    "Curve sharpens at wp27-35 (y increases from 1.0 to 2.0), monitor delta closely",
    "Car heading increasing from ~0° to ~19° confirming we are deep in the left curve",
    "Delta ~25° at wp30 confirms moderate-sharp left curve — maintain 15° left steering",
    "Heading increasing steadily each step (~5°/step) — curve is consistent"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [6.3516, 6.4394, 6.5327, 6.6233],
    "y": [0.6949, 0.7256, 0.7702, 0.8196],
    "yaw": [5.724, 9.628, 14.818, 19.168]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 25.33,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 15.0
}
```
